basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: al-Ḥadīd — “Iron” — opens on universal glorification and the litany of Names: the First and the Last, the Manifest and the Hidden. Qushayrī opens with an inebriated basmala: the hearing of “Bismillāh” is a wine with which the Real gives His beloved's hearts to drink; when they drink, they are enraptured; enraptured, they expand; then they expose themselves to the contemplation of His truth, find calm in the breath of His nearness, and become absent when they perceive themselves. He unfolds upon “He is the First and the Last, the Manifest and the Hidden” a vast cascade of meanings, and makes of the verse “no calamity strikes save that it is in a Book before We bring it about” the root of trusting surrender and detachment: not to grieve over what escapes nor rejoice excessively over what is given is the mark of those freed from the yoke of the soul. The sūra calls to the goodly loan (qarḍ ḥasan), paints the light running before the believers and the wall that separates them from the hypocrites, denounces this world as “play, idle talk, vain adornment,” and reveals that iron was “sent down,” bearing a fearsome force and benefits for mankind.
themes: the First and the Last, the Manifest and the Hidden
the inscribed calamity and the tranquil soul

versets.ar: سَبَّحَ لِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۖ وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: sabbaḥa lillāhi mā fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-huwa l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: All that is in the heavens and the earth glorifies God, and He is the Mighty, the Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: tasbīḥ is sanctification and transcendence; it is also the swimming of secrets in the seas of majesty, where they seize the jewels of tawḥīd, string them into necklaces of faith, and set them in the torques of union. And he notes that “what” (mā) embraces every thing: no being, no trace that does not point to the Maker and attest His right to greatness.

mots.ar: سَبَّحَ لِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ
mots.tr: sabbaḥa lillāhi mā fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍ
mots.racine: س ب ح
mots.sens: all in heavens and earth glorifies God

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ح ك م
mots.sens: the Mighty, the Wise

versets.ar: لَهُ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۖ يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ ۖ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: lahu mulku s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi yuḥyī wa-yumītu wa-huwa ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīrun
versets.trad: His is the Kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He gives life and gives death, and He is powerful over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: none possesses (mālik) but God; when it is said of another, it is by metaphor. And he interiorizes “He gives life and gives death”: He vivifies souls and makes them die; He vivifies hearts by turning toward them, and makes them die by turning away; He vivifies them by His gaze and grace, makes them die by His coercion and might.

mots.racine: م ل ك
mots.sens: His is the Kingdom of heavens and earth

mots.racine: ح ي ي / م و ت
mots.sens: He gives life and death
mots.def: aḥyā: to vivify; amāta: to make die.

versets.ar: هُوَ الْأَوَّلُ وَالْآخِرُ وَالظَّاهِرُ وَالْبَاطِنُ ۖ وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: huwa l-awwalu wa-l-ākhiru wa-ẓ-ẓāhiru wa-l-bāṭinu wa-huwa bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīmun
versets.trad: He is the First and the Last, the Manifest and the Hidden, and He is the Knower of all things.
versets.commentaire: The verse of the four Names, on which Qushayrī unfolds a dizzying cascade: the First without beginning, the Last without end, the Manifest without veil, the Hidden without access to His truth; the First through providence, the Last through guidance, the Manifest through protection, the Hidden through sanctity. And he makes of it a path: he whom the Name “the First” dominates meditates on his predestination; he whom “the Last” dominates on his end; “the Manifest” leads him to gratitude for present favours; “the Hidden” to trembling before the unknown of his fate.

mots.racine: أ و ل / أ خ ر
mots.sens: He is the First and the Last

mots.racine: ظ ه ر / ب ط ن
mots.sens: the Manifest and the Hidden

mots.racine: ع ل م
mots.sens: He knows all things

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ اسْتَوَىٰ عَلَى الْعَرْشِ ۚ يَعْلَمُ مَا يَلِجُ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَمَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا وَمَا يَنزِلُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ وَمَا يَعْرُجُ فِيهَا ۖ وَهُوَ مَعَكُمْ أَيْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ ۚ وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī khalaqa s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa fī sittati ayyāmin thumma stawā ʿalā l-ʿarshi yaʿlamu mā yaliju fī l-arḍi wa-mā yakhruju minhā wa-mā yanzilu mina s-samāʾi wa-mā yaʿruju fīhā wa-huwa maʿakum ayna mā kuntum wa-llāhu bimā taʿmalūna baṣīrun
versets.trad: It is He who created the heavens and the earth in six days, then established Himself in majesty upon the Throne. He knows what enters the earth and what comes out of it, what descends from the sky and what ascends into it. And He is with you wherever you are. And God sees perfectly all that you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī interiorizes each movement: “what enters the earth” — when the servant is buried, God knows the sincerity or doubt that was in his heart; “what descends from the sky” — upon the hearts of His saints, the subtle graces, the unveilings, the precious states; “what ascends into it” — the breaths of the saints when they rise, their sighs when they mount. And “He is with you wherever you are”: by knowledge and power — the omnipresence of the Witness.

mots.racine: س و ي / ع ر ش
mots.sens: then He established Himself upon the Throne

mots.racine: ع ل م / و ل ج
mots.sens: He knows what enters the earth

mots.racine: ك و ن
mots.sens: He is with you wherever you are

versets.ar: لَّهُ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۚ وَإِلَى اللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ الْأُمُورُ
versets.tr: lahu mulku s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-ilā llāhi turjaʿu l-umūru
versets.trad: His is the Kingdom of the heavens and the earth. To God all matters return.
versets.commentaire: A reaffirmation of the Kingdom, sealed by the return of every matter (al-umūr) to God. All proceeds from Him and all returns to Him: the circle of existence closes in His hand. The servant who knows this ceases to dispute the course of things and renders it to his Master.

mots.racine: م ل ك
mots.sens: His is the Kingdom of heavens and earth

mots.racine: ر ج ع / أ م ر
mots.sens: to God matters return

versets.ar: يُولِجُ اللَّيْلَ فِي النَّهَارِ وَيُولِجُ النَّهَارَ فِي اللَّيْلِ ۚ وَهُوَ عَلِيمٌ بِذَاتِ الصُّدُورِ
versets.tr: yūliju l-layla fī n-nahāri wa-yūliju n-nahāra fī l-layli wa-huwa ʿalīmun bi-dhāti ṣ-ṣudūri
versets.trad: He makes the night enter the day and the day enter the night. And He knows perfectly the inmost of hearts.
themes: the alternation of day and night
versets.commentaire: The interweaving of day and night (yūliju) figures the mastery of time and of every transition. And the verse closes on the knowledge of the breasts' content (dhāt al-ṣudūr): He who makes night pass into day also makes light pass into hearts and knows their most buried secret.

mots.racine: و ل ج / ل ي ل / ن ه ر
mots.sens: He makes the night enter the day
mots.def: awlaja: to make enter; layl: night; nahār: day.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ص د ر
mots.sens: He knows the content of the breasts

versets.ar: آمِنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَأَنفِقُوا مِمَّا جَعَلَكُم مُّسْتَخْلَفِينَ فِيهِ ۖ فَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنكُمْ وَأَنفَقُوا لَهُمْ أَجْرٌ كَبِيرٌ
versets.tr: āminū billāhi wa-rasūlihi wa-anfiqū mimmā jaʿalakum mustakhlafīna fīhi fa-lladhīna āmanū minkum wa-anfaqū lahum ajrun kabīrun
versets.trad: Believe in God and His Messenger! Spend in alms of that of which He has made you trustees. For those of you who believe and give alms there is a great reward.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī dwells on “that of which He has made you trustees” (mustakhlafīn): wealth is no property but a stewardship. What the hands hold is exposed to passing away: the happy one is he who has placed his wealth in advance in the prosperity of his Hereafter; the wretched one is he who walked toward the ruin of his fate. To spend is to render what was only entrusted.

mots.racine: ن ف ق / خ ل ف
mots.sens: spend of that wherein He made you trustees

mots.racine: أ ج ر / ك ب ر
mots.sens: they have a great reward

versets.ar: وَمَا لَكُمْ لَا تُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ ۙ وَالرَّسُولُ يَدْعُوكُمْ لِتُؤْمِنُوا بِرَبِّكُمْ وَقَدْ أَخَذَ مِيثَاقَكُمْ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā lakum lā tuʾminūna billāhi wa-r-rasūlu yadʿūkum li-tuʾminū bi-rabbikum wa-qad akhadha mīthāqakum in kuntum muʾminīna
versets.trad: Why would you not believe in God, when the Messenger calls you to believe in your Lord and He has taken a covenant from you, if you are believers?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: what pretext have you for forsaking faith, when He has dispelled every excuse by making the proof shine for you, and has taken your pledge on the Day of the primordial Covenant (yawm al-dharr)? Faith is no novelty but the recollection of a prior oath: to believe is to honour a covenant already sealed in the secret of origins.

mots.racine: أ م ن
mots.sens: what ails you that you do not believe in God

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / و ث ق
mots.sens: He has taken your covenant

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي يُنَزِّلُ عَلَىٰ عَبْدِهِ آيَاتٍ بَيِّنَاتٍ لِّيُخْرِجَكُم مِّنَ الظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَى النُّورِ ۚ وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ بِكُمْ لَرَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī yunazzilu ʿalā ʿabdihi āyātin bayyinātin li-yukhrijakum mina ẓ-ẓulumāti ilā n-nūri wa-inna llāha bikum la-raʾūfun raḥīmun
versets.trad: It is He who sends down to His servant clear verses to bring you out of the darkness into the light. God, indeed, is kind and compassionate to you!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī widens the exit from darkness: from the darkness of ignorance to the light of knowledge, from the darkness of doubt to the light of certainty; He shows them signs in themselves through the unveilings of the secret, brings them out of the darkness of anxious self-management (tadbīr) into the breadth of surrender to God (tafwīḍ). When the soul leans toward ease, whoever hastens to what the Reality demands finds in his heart a light by which he knows the darkness of the soul's suggestions.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / أ ي ي / ب ي ن
mots.sens: He sends down to His servant clear signs

mots.racine: خ ر ج / ظ ل م / ن و ر
mots.sens: to bring you from darkness into light

versets.ar: وَمَا لَكُمْ أَلَّا تُنفِقُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَلِلَّهِ مِيرَاثُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۚ لَا يَسْتَوِي مِنكُم مَّنْ أَنفَقَ مِن قَبْلِ الْفَتْحِ وَقَاتَلَ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ أَعْظَمُ دَرَجَةً مِّنَ الَّذِينَ أَنفَقُوا مِن بَعْدُ وَقَاتَلُوا ۚ وَكُلًّا وَعَدَ اللَّهُ الْحُسْنَىٰ ۚ وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-mā lakum allā tunfiqū fī sabīli llāhi wa-lillāhi mīrāthu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi lā yastawī minkum man anfaqa min qabli l-fatḥi wa-qātala ulāʾika aʿẓamu darajatan mina lladhīna anfaqū min baʿdu wa-qātalū wa-kullan waʿada llāhu l-ḥusnā wa-llāhu bimā taʿmalūna khabīrun
versets.trad: Why would you not spend in the way of God, when to God belongs the inheritance of the heavens and the earth? You are not all equal: among you are those who spent and fought before the victory; those are higher in rank than those who spent and fought afterward. Yet to all God has promised the finest reward; and God is aware of all that you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: what your hands hold returns to God as inheritance; soon it will pass to others and you will not remain despite the length of your hopes. And he praises precedence: those who gave before the victory (al-fatḥ) are of higher rank, for the people's need was then greater and the matter harder. He cites: “outstrip, outstrip, in word and deed; warn your soul of the laggard's regret.”

mots.racine: و ر ث
mots.sens: to God the inheritance of heavens and earth

mots.ar: مَّنْ أَنفَقَ مِن قَبْلِ الْفَتْحِ وَقَاتَلَ
mots.tr: man anfaqa min qabli l-fatḥi wa-qātala
mots.racine: ن ف ق / ف ت ح / ق ت ل
mots.sens: who spent and fought before the victory

mots.racine: و ع د / ح س ن
mots.sens: to all God promised the finest reward

versets.ar: مَّن ذَا الَّذِي يُقْرِضُ اللَّهَ قَرْضًا حَسَنًا فَيُضَاعِفَهُ لَهُ وَلَهُ أَجْرٌ كَرِيمٌ
versets.tr: man dhā lladhī yuqriḍu llāha qarḍan ḥasanan fa-yuḍāʿifahu lahu wa-lahu ajrun karīmun
versets.trad: Who is it that will lend God a goodly loan, that He may multiply it for him and that he may have a generous reward?
versets.commentaire: The goodly loan (qarḍ ḥasan). Qushayrī: He named almsgiving a loan to set their hearts at ease. And he defines the loan's goodliness: that it be from lawful gain, from a glad heart, made with sincerity, without riyāʾ, without humiliating reminder to the poor, without tarnishing by delay, without awaiting abundant return. And higher: to lend by cutting from your heart the love of the two abodes — for “the best alms is that which leaves an ease behind it.”

mots.ar: مَّن ذَا الَّذِي يُقْرِضُ اللَّهَ قَرْضًا حَسَنًا
mots.tr: man dhā lladhī yuqriḍu llāha qarḍan ḥasanan
mots.racine: ق ر ض / ح س ن
mots.sens: who will lend God a goodly loan

mots.racine: ض ع ف
mots.sens: that He may multiply it for him

mots.racine: أ ج ر / ك ر م
mots.sens: he shall have a generous reward

versets.ar: يَوْمَ تَرَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ يَسْعَىٰ نُورُهُم بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَبِأَيْمَانِهِم بُشْرَاكُمُ الْيَوْمَ جَنَّاتٌ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ
versets.tr: yawma tarā l-muʾminīna wa-l-muʾmināti yasʿā nūruhum bayna aydīhim wa-bi-aymānihim bushrākumu l-yawma jannātun tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru khālidīna fīhā dhālika huwa l-fawzu l-ʿaẓīmu
versets.trad: On a Day you will see the believing men and women, their light running before them and on their right. “Good tidings for you today: Gardens beneath which rivers flow, where you will abide forever. That is the supreme triumph!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it is a light given to the believers to the measure of their fair deeds; it has rays they follow, it runs before them and surrounds them on every side. And he joins to it the inner light already here below: today already, they have in their hearts a light by which they walk and are guided in all their states — “the believer sees by the light of God.” This light can even radiate upon whoever draws near them.

mots.racine: س ع ي / ن و ر
mots.sens: their light runs before them

mots.racine: ب ش ر / ج ن ن
mots.sens: your good tidings today: Gardens

mots.racine: ف و ز / ع ظ م
mots.sens: that is the supreme triumph

versets.ar: يَوْمَ يَقُولُ الْمُنَافِقُونَ وَالْمُنَافِقَاتُ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا انظُرُونَا نَقْتَبِسْ مِن نُّورِكُمْ قِيلَ ارْجِعُوا وَرَاءَكُمْ فَالْتَمِسُوا نُورًا فَضُرِبَ بَيْنَهُم بِسُورٍ لَّهُ بَابٌ بَاطِنُهُ فِيهِ الرَّحْمَةُ وَظَاهِرُهُ مِن قِبَلِهِ الْعَذَابُ
versets.tr: yawma yaqūlu l-munāfiqūna wa-l-munāfiqātu lilladhīna āmanū nẓurūnā naqtabis min nūrikum qīla rjiʿū warāʾakum fa-ltamisū nūran fa-ḍuriba baynahum bi-sūrin lahu bābun bāṭinuhu fīhi r-raḥmatu wa-ẓāhiruhu min qibalihi l-ʿadhābu
versets.trad: On that Day the hypocrite men and women will say to the believers: “Wait for us, that we may borrow from your light!” It will be said: “Go back behind you and seek a light!” Then a wall will be set up between them, with a gate whose inner side holds mercy and whose outer side faces the punishment.
themes: the light denied to the hypocrites
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: believers and hypocrites receive their books in the light; but on walking, the light goes out before the hypocrites and the believers outstrip. To “go back and seek a light” he gives a dizzying sense: go back to the decree of eternity and seek it in the primordial allotment! — a way of saying the impossible. The wall (sūr) with the double-sided gate — within, mercy; without, punishment — separates the two destinies forever.

mots.racine: ن ظ ر / ق ب س / ن و ر
mots.sens: wait for us, that we may borrow from your light
mots.def: iqtabasa: to take a flame, borrow light.

mots.racine: ر ج ع / ل م س
mots.sens: go back behind and seek a light

mots.ar: بَاطِنُهُ فِيهِ الرَّحْمَةُ وَظَاهِرُهُ مِن قِبَلِهِ الْعَذَابُ
mots.tr: bāṭinuhu fīhi r-raḥmatu wa-ẓāhiruhu min qibalihi l-ʿadhāb
mots.racine: ب ط ن / ظ ه ر / ر ح م / ع ذ ب
mots.sens: within is mercy, without is punishment

versets.ar: يُنَادُونَهُمْ أَلَمْ نَكُن مَّعَكُمْ ۖ قَالُوا بَلَىٰ وَلَٰكِنَّكُمْ فَتَنتُمْ أَنفُسَكُمْ وَتَرَبَّصْتُمْ وَارْتَبْتُمْ وَغَرَّتْكُمُ الْأَمَانِيُّ حَتَّىٰ جَاءَ أَمْرُ اللَّهِ وَغَرَّكُم بِاللَّهِ الْغَرُورُ
versets.tr: yunādūnahum a-lam nakun maʿakum qālū balā wa-lākinnakum fatantum anfusakum wa-tarabbaṣtum wa-rtabtum wa-gharratkumu l-amāniyyu ḥattā jāʾa amru llāhi wa-gharrakum billāhi l-gharūru
versets.trad: They will call out to them: “Were we not with you?” They will reply: “Yes, but you tempted your own souls, you bided your time and doubted; vain desires deluded you until God's decree came, and the deceiver deluded you about God.”
versets.commentaire: “Were we not with you?” — “Yes, but you tempted your souls (fatantum anfusakum), bided your time, doubted.” Qushayrī: you tarried far from sincerity, you doubted, the devil lured you, and you leaned toward this world. Outer company does not save: one was “with” the believers of bodies, not of hearts — and the deceiver (al-gharūr) filled the void.

mots.racine: ك و ن
mots.sens: were we not with you?

mots.racine: ف ت ن / ن ف س
mots.sens: but you tempted your souls

mots.racine: غ ر ر
mots.sens: the deceiver deluded you about God

versets.ar: فَالْيَوْمَ لَا يُؤْخَذُ مِنكُمْ فِدْيَةٌ وَلَا مِنَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ۚ مَأْوَاكُمُ النَّارُ ۖ هِيَ مَوْلَاكُمْ ۖ وَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: fa-l-yawma lā yuʾkhadhu minkum fidyatun wa-lā mina lladhīna kafarū maʾwākumu n-nāru hiya mawlākum wa-biʾsa l-maṣīru
versets.trad: So today no ransom is accepted from you, nor from those who disbelieved. Your refuge is the Fire; it is your master! What a wretched destination!
versets.commentaire: The Fire is their refuge and their “master” (mawlā) — by bitter irony: it is more fitting for you, says Qushayrī. And he concludes on the unmasking of the hypocrites: the discordance of consciences and secrets is not hidden under the accord of appearances; secrets do not stay veiled at the test. On the Day when no ransom is accepted, the inside of hearts appears as it was.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ف د ي
mots.sens: no ransom is taken from you

mots.racine: أ و ي / ن و ر / و ل ي
mots.sens: your refuge is the Fire, it is your master

versets.ar: أَلَمْ يَأْنِ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَن تَخْشَعَ قُلُوبُهُمْ لِذِكْرِ اللَّهِ وَمَا نَزَلَ مِنَ الْحَقِّ وَلَا يَكُونُوا كَالَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ مِن قَبْلُ فَطَالَ عَلَيْهِمُ الْأَمَدُ فَقَسَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ ۖ وَكَثِيرٌ مِّنْهُمْ فَاسِقُونَ
versets.tr: a-lam yaʾni lilladhīna āmanū an takhshaʿa qulūbuhum li-dhikri llāhi wa-mā nazala mina l-ḥaqqi wa-lā yakūnū ka-lladhīna ūtū l-kitāba min qablu fa-ṭāla ʿalayhimu l-amadu fa-qasat qulūbuhum wa-kathīrun minhum fāsiqūna
versets.trad: Has the time not come for the believers' hearts to be humbled at the remembrance of God and the Truth that has come down, and not to be like those given the Scripture before, for whom the time grew long, so their hearts hardened and many of them became corrupt?
themes: the danger of the hardened heart
versets.commentaire: An overwhelming verse: “Has the time not come for hearts to be humbled at God's remembrance?” Qushayrī analyses the genesis of the heart's hardness: it comes from following passion; covetousness and purity do not cohabit. Its origin is the heart's swerving from vigilance of the Lord. It begins with a passing thought; if unchecked, it becomes an idea; then a resolve; then a transgression; then, for want of repair, hardness; and finally a seal and rust (rayn). Hardening is forestalled at its first glimmer.

mots.ar: أَلَمْ يَأْنِ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَن تَخْشَعَ قُلُوبُهُمْ
mots.tr: a-lam yaʾni lilladhīna āmanū an takhshaʿa qulūbuhum
mots.racine: أ ن ي / خ ش ع
mots.sens: has the time not come for their hearts to be humbled
mots.def: ʾanā: to be time; khashaʿa: to be humbled.

mots.racine: ط و ل / أ م د / ق س و
mots.sens: the time grew long, their hearts hardened

versets.ar: اعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ يُحْيِي الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا ۚ قَدْ بَيَّنَّا لَكُمُ الْآيَاتِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: iʿlamū anna llāha yuḥyī l-arḍa baʿda mawtihā qad bayyannā lakumu l-āyāti laʿallakum taʿqilūna
versets.trad: Know that God revives the earth after its death. We have made clear to you the signs, that you may understand.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of the verse a balm after the warning on the hardened heart: He revives the dead earth by rain and vegetation — and He revives the dead hearts, after turning away from them, by the beauty of His turning back toward them. The most withered heart can grow green again: the same power that revives the barren earth revives the hardened soul.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / م و ت
mots.sens: He revives the earth after its death

mots.racine: ب ي ن / أ ي ي
mots.sens: We have made clear to you the signs
mots.def: bayyana: to make clear; āyāt: signs.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الْمُصَّدِّقِينَ وَالْمُصَّدِّقَاتِ وَأَقْرَضُوا اللَّهَ قَرْضًا حَسَنًا يُضَاعَفُ لَهُمْ وَلَهُمْ أَجْرٌ كَرِيمٌ
versets.tr: inna l-muṣṣaddiqīna wa-l-muṣṣaddiqāti wa-aqraḍū llāha qarḍan ḥasanan yuḍāʿafu lahum wa-lahum ajrun karīmun
versets.trad: Truly, the charitable men and women, and those who lend God a goodly loan — it will be multiplied for them, and they will have a generous reward.
versets.commentaire: A reprise of the goodly loan, widened to the charitable men and women. Qushayrī: the multiplication (yuḍāʿaf) runs from the good deed multiplied tenfold up to what God wills. And the “generous reward” (ajr karīm): God is not stingy with the highest wage for obedience, however small — divine generosity always surpasses the measure of the human gift.

mots.racine: ص د ق
mots.sens: the charitable men and women

mots.racine: ض ع ف
mots.sens: it is multiplied for them

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِ أُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الصِّدِّيقُونَ ۖ وَالشُّهَدَاءُ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ لَهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ وَنُورُهُمْ ۖ وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَكَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ الْجَحِيمِ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna āmanū billāhi wa-rusulihi ulāʾika humu ṣ-ṣiddīqūna wa-sh-shuhadāʾu ʿinda rabbihim lahum ajruhum wa-nūruhum wa-lladhīna kafarū wa-kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā ulāʾika aṣḥābu l-jaḥīmi
versets.trad: Those who believe in God and His messengers — those are the truthful ones; and the witnesses before their Lord have their reward and their light. But those who disbelieved and denied Our signs are the inhabitants of the Blaze.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī defines the degrees: the ṣiddīq is one whose outer and inner are equal; he is also one who bears the matter to its most exacting, descends not to easy ways, leans not toward accommodations. The witnesses (shuhadāʾ) are those who contemplate by their hearts the abodes of union, who withdraw by their secrets into the homelands of nearness; and their light is that with which the Real has anointed their inner sight: the lights of tawḥīd.

mots.racine: ص د ق
mots.sens: those are the truthful

mots.racine: ش ه د
mots.sens: and the witnesses before their Lord

mots.racine: أ ج ر / ن و ر
mots.sens: theirs is their reward and their light

versets.ar: اعْلَمُوا أَنَّمَا الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ وَزِينَةٌ وَتَفَاخُرٌ بَيْنَكُمْ وَتَكَاثُرٌ فِي الْأَمْوَالِ وَالْأَوْلَادِ ۖ كَمَثَلِ غَيْثٍ أَعْجَبَ الْكُفَّارَ نَبَاتُهُ ثُمَّ يَهِيجُ فَتَرَاهُ مُصْفَرًّا ثُمَّ يَكُونُ حُطَامًا ۖ وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ عَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌ وَمَغْفِرَةٌ مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَرِضْوَانٌ ۚ وَمَا الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا إِلَّا مَتَاعُ الْغُرُورِ
versets.tr: iʿlamū annamā l-ḥayātu d-dunyā laʿibun wa-lahwun wa-zīnatun wa-tafākhurun baynakum wa-takāthurun fī l-amwāli wa-l-awlādi ka-mathali ghaythin aʿjaba l-kuffāra nabātuhu thumma yahīju fa-tarāhu muṣfarran thumma yakūnu ḥuṭāman wa-fī l-ākhirati ʿadhābun shadīdun wa-maghfiratun mina llāhi wa-riḍwānun wa-mā l-ḥayātu d-dunyā illā matāʿu l-ghurūri
versets.trad: Know that the life of this world is but play, idle talk, vain adornment, mutual boasting among you, and rivalry in wealth and children. It is like a rain whose growth delights the tillers; then it withers, you see it turn yellow, and it becomes dry chaff. In the Hereafter there is a severe punishment and a forgiveness from God with His good pleasure. The life of this world is but the enjoyment of delusion.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paints this world: exposed to vanishing, neither lasting nor abiding, turning one from God, alluring without satisfying; it runs off the paths of rectitude like children's play. And he settles it: this world is base — baser still is he who seeks it, and more contemptible he who quarrels over it; it is but a carcass, and the seeker of a carcass has no worth. The worst of the people of this world is he who is miserly with it. The “blamed world” is the one that turns one from the Hereafter.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / ل ع ب / ل ه و / ز ي ن
mots.sens: the life of this world is play, idle talk, adornment
mots.def: laʿib: play; lahw: idle talk; zīna: adornment.

mots.racine: غ ي ث / ع ج ب / ن ب ت
mots.sens: like rain whose growth delights the tillers
mots.def: ghayth: rain; kuffār: tillers (lit. who cover the seed).

mots.ar: وَمَا الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا إِلَّا مَتَاعُ الْغُرُورِ
mots.tr: wa-mā l-ḥayātu d-dunyā illā matāʿu l-ghurūr
mots.racine: م ت ع / غ ر ر
mots.sens: the life of this world is but the enjoyment of delusion

versets.ar: سَابِقُوا إِلَىٰ مَغْفِرَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَجَنَّةٍ عَرْضُهَا كَعَرْضِ السَّمَاءِ وَالْأَرْضِ أُعِدَّتْ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ فَضْلُ اللَّهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَن يَشَاءُ ۚ وَاللَّهُ ذُو الْفَضْلِ الْعَظِيمِ
versets.tr: sābiqū ilā maghfiratin min rabbikum wa-jannatin ʿarḍuhā ka-ʿarḍi s-samāʾi wa-l-arḍi uʿiddat lilladhīna āmanū billāhi wa-rusulihi dhālika faḍlu llāhi yuʾtīhi man yashāʾu wa-llāhu dhū l-faḍli l-ʿaẓīmi
versets.trad: Race toward forgiveness from your Lord and a Garden whose breadth is as the breadth of the heaven and the earth, prepared for those who believed in God and His messengers. That is the grace of God; He gives it to whom He wills. God is the Possessor of immense grace!
versets.commentaire: “Race” (sābiqū) toward forgiveness. Qushayrī: hasten toward the deed that earns you forgiveness — and that deed is repentance. He notes that only the Garden's breadth is mentioned: the length is to the measure that answers the breadth. And on “that is the grace of God”: a refutation of whoever claims to merit Paradise by his deeds — grace cannot be a due. And he adds: when the believers' secrets heard this call, the spirits rushed forth, demanding of the limbs their haste.

mots.racine: س ب ق / غ ف ر
mots.sens: race toward forgiveness from your Lord
mots.def: sābaqa: to vie in speed; maghfira: forgiveness.

mots.racine: ع ر ض
mots.sens: its breadth as the breadth of heaven and earth

mots.ar: ذَٰلِكَ فَضْلُ اللَّهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَن يَشَاءُ
mots.tr: dhālika faḍlu llāhi yuʾtīhi man yashāʾ
mots.racine: ف ض ل
mots.sens: that is God's grace, He gives it to whom He wills

versets.ar: مَا أَصَابَ مِن مُّصِيبَةٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي أَنفُسِكُمْ إِلَّا فِي كِتَابٍ مِّن قَبْلِ أَن نَّبْرَأَهَا ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى اللَّهِ يَسِيرٌ
versets.tr: mā aṣāba min muṣībatin fī l-arḍi wa-lā fī anfusikum illā fī kitābin min qabli an nabraʾahā inna dhālika ʿalā llāhi yasīrun
versets.trad: No calamity strikes the earth or yourselves but it is in a Book before We bring it about. That, for God, is easy!
versets.commentaire: Every calamity (muṣība) is inscribed in the Book before it comes about. Qushayrī draws an unsuspected sweetness from it: the servant, knowing that what strikes him — expansion, repose, the heart's influx — comes from God, feels the keenest joy and the fullest intimacy, for he knows he was singled out for it in the secret, from the very depth of non-being. He cites: “Blessed be the abode that, had it not been, my heart would never have known passion.”

mots.racine: ص و ب
mots.sens: no calamity strikes

mots.ar: إِلَّا فِي كِتَابٍ مِّن قَبْلِ أَن نَّبْرَأَهَا
mots.tr: illā fī kitābin min qabli an nabraʾahā
mots.racine: ك ت ب / ب ر أ
mots.sens: but it is in a Book before We bring it about

mots.racine: ي س ر
mots.sens: that is easy for God

versets.ar: لِّكَيْلَا تَأْسَوْا عَلَىٰ مَا فَاتَكُمْ وَلَا تَفْرَحُوا بِمَا آتَاكُمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ مُخْتَالٍ فَخُورٍ
versets.tr: li-kaylā taʾsaw ʿalā mā fātakum wa-lā tafraḥū bimā ātākum wa-llāhu lā yuḥibbu kulla mukhtālin fakhūrin
versets.trad: So that you may not grieve over what escapes you nor exult over what He gives you. God loves not any arrogant boaster,
versets.commentaire: The fruit of the prior verse: neither grief over what escapes nor overflowing joy at what is given. Qushayrī: not to rejoice at what one receives is the attribute of those freed from the yoke of the soul. The worth of men is revealed in their changing: he who does not change at what displeases him is accomplished; he who changes neither for fortune nor for misfortune, whom existence does not gladden nor lack sadden — that one is the master of his moment (sayyid waqtih).

mots.racine: أ س و / ف و ت
mots.sens: that you grieve not over what escapes you
mots.def: asiya: to grieve; fāta: to escape.

mots.racine: ف ر ح / أ ت ي
mots.sens: nor exult over what He gives you

mots.racine: خ ي ل / ف خ ر
mots.sens: every arrogant boaster

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يَبْخَلُونَ وَيَأْمُرُونَ النَّاسَ بِالْبُخْلِ ۗ وَمَن يَتَوَلَّ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ الْغَنِيُّ الْحَمِيدُ
versets.tr: alladhīna yabkhalūna wa-yaʾmurūna n-nāsa bi-l-bukhli wa-man yatawalla fa-inna llāha huwa l-ghaniyyu l-ḥamīdu
versets.trad: those who are miserly and bid people to miserliness. Whoever turns away — God is the Self-Sufficient, the Praiseworthy!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes the jurist's miserliness from the spiritual's: miserliness, in the language of the law, is to withhold the due (the obligatory zakāt); but according to this community [the Sufis], it has been said: miserliness is to see a worth in things; the miser is one who gives only at the asking; and it has been said: whoever engraves his name on his ring is a miser — for he should be ready to yield his property at any instant. And whoever turns away wrongs only himself: God is al-Ghaniyy, the absolute Rich.

mots.racine: ب خ ل / أ م ر
mots.sens: those who are miserly and bid miserliness
mots.def: bakhila: to be miserly; bukhl: miserliness.

mots.racine: غ ن ي / ح م د
mots.sens: God is the Self-Sufficient, the Praiseworthy
mots.def: ghaniyy: sufficient unto Himself; ḥamīd: praiseworthy.

versets.ar: لَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا رُسُلَنَا بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ وَأَنزَلْنَا مَعَهُمُ الْكِتَابَ وَالْمِيزَانَ لِيَقُومَ النَّاسُ بِالْقِسْطِ ۖ وَأَنزَلْنَا الْحَدِيدَ فِيهِ بَأْسٌ شَدِيدٌ وَمَنَافِعُ لِلنَّاسِ وَلِيَعْلَمَ اللَّهُ مَن يَنصُرُهُ وَرُسُلَهُ بِالْغَيْبِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ قَوِيٌّ عَزِيزٌ
versets.tr: laqad arsalnā rusulanā bi-l-bayyināti wa-anzalnā maʿahumu l-kitāba wa-l-mīzāna li-yaqūma n-nāsu bi-l-qisṭi wa-anzalnā l-ḥadīda fīhi baʾsun shadīdun wa-manāfiʿu lin-nāsi wa-li-yaʿlama llāhu man yanṣuruhu wa-rusulahu bi-l-ghaybi inna llāha qawiyyun ʿazīzun
versets.trad: We sent Our messengers with clear proofs, and We sent down with them the Scripture and the balance, so that people might uphold justice. And We sent down iron, in which is fearsome force and benefits for mankind, that God may know who helps Him and His messengers in the unseen. Truly, God is Strong, Mighty!
versets.commentaire: The verse that gives the sūra its name. Three descents: the Scripture, the balance (just judgment), and iron (al-ḥadīd). Qushayrī: We sent Our messengers supported by dazzling proofs, removing every pretext from whoever wished to follow the straightest way; We sent down the Books, and the balance — that is, judgment by the Qurʾān, the observance of justice and equality among men. Iron bears “a fearsome force and benefits”: the instrument of just war as of useful industry, given to test who helps God in the unseen.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / ك ت ب / و ز ن
mots.sens: We sent down the Scripture and the balance

mots.racine: ن ز ل / ح د د / ب أ س
mots.sens: We sent down iron, fearsome force
mots.def: ḥadīd: iron; baʾs: force, warlike might.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ن ص ر / غ ي ب
mots.sens: that God may know who helps Him in the unseen
mots.def: naṣara: to help; ghayb: unseen, secret.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا وَإِبْرَاهِيمَ وَجَعَلْنَا فِي ذُرِّيَّتِهِمَا النُّبُوَّةَ وَالْكِتَابَ ۖ فَمِنْهُم مُّهْتَدٍ ۖ وَكَثِيرٌ مِّنْهُمْ فَاسِقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad arsalnā nūḥan wa-ibrāhīma wa-jaʿalnā fī dhurriyyatihimā n-nubuwwata wa-l-kitāba fa-minhum muhtadin wa-kathīrun minhum fāsiqūna
versets.trad: We sent Noah and Abraham and placed prophethood and the Scripture among their descendants. Among them are some rightly guided, but many of them are corrupt.
versets.commentaire: From Noah and Abraham, God brought forth prophethood (nubuwwa) and the Scripture. But the noble descent is no due: among them, the guided (muhtadin) and many corrupt (fāsiqūn). The grace of lineage does not entail salvation: each soul answers for itself, and prophetic blood does not save whoever turns from it.

mots.racine: ذ ر ر / ن ب أ / ك ت ب
mots.sens: We placed in their descendants prophethood and Scripture

mots.racine: ه د ي / ف س ق
mots.sens: among them are guided ones and many corrupt

versets.ar: ثُمَّ قَفَّيْنَا عَلَىٰ آثَارِهِم بِرُسُلِنَا وَقَفَّيْنَا بِعِيسَى ابْنِ مَرْيَمَ وَآتَيْنَاهُ الْإِنجِيلَ وَجَعَلْنَا فِي قُلُوبِ الَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُ رَأْفَةً وَرَحْمَةً وَرَهْبَانِيَّةً ابْتَدَعُوهَا مَا كَتَبْنَاهَا عَلَيْهِمْ إِلَّا ابْتِغَاءَ رِضْوَانِ اللَّهِ فَمَا رَعَوْهَا حَقَّ رِعَايَتِهَا ۖ فَآتَيْنَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنْهُمْ أَجْرَهُمْ ۖ وَكَثِيرٌ مِّنْهُمْ فَاسِقُونَ
versets.tr: thumma qaffaynā ʿalā āthārihim bi-rusulinā wa-qaffaynā bi-ʿīsā bni maryama wa-ātaynāhu l-injīla wa-jaʿalnā fī qulūbi lladhīna ttabaʿūhu raʾfatan wa-raḥmatan wa-rahbāniyyatan ibtadaʿūhā mā katabnāhā ʿalayhim illā btighāʾa riḍwāni llāhi fa-mā raʿawhā ḥaqqa riʿāyatihā fa-ātaynā lladhīna āmanū minhum ajrahum wa-kathīrun minhum fāsiqūna
versets.trad: Then We sent Our messengers in their footsteps, and We sent Jesus, son of Mary, and gave him the Gospel. We placed in the hearts of those who followed him kindness, compassion, and a monasticism they invented — We did not prescribe it to them — only seeking God's good pleasure, but they did not observe it as it should have been. To those of them who believed We gave their reward, but many of them are corrupt.
versets.commentaire: God placed in the hearts of Jesus' followers kindness (raʾfa), compassion (raḥma) — and a monasticism (rahbāniyya) they invented. Qushayrī clarifies: God had not prescribed the rahbāniyya; it is they who instaurated it, seeking His good pleasure — but they did not observe it as they should. A twofold lesson: the impulse toward God may invent sincere ways, but no improvised devotion avails if it is not held to the end.

mots.ar: وَجَعَلْنَا فِي قُلُوبِ الَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُ رَأْفَةً وَرَحْمَةً
mots.tr: wa-jaʿalnā fī qulūbi lladhīna ttabaʿūhu raʾfatan wa-raḥma
mots.racine: ر أ ف / ر ح م
mots.sens: We placed in their hearts kindness and compassion

mots.racine: ر ه ب / ب د ع
mots.sens: and a monasticism they invented

mots.racine: ر ع ي
mots.sens: they did not observe it as it should be

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَآمِنُوا بِرَسُولِهِ يُؤْتِكُمْ كِفْلَيْنِ مِن رَّحْمَتِهِ وَيَجْعَل لَّكُمْ نُورًا تَمْشُونَ بِهِ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ۚ وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū ttaqū llāha wa-āminū bi-rasūlihi yuʾtikum kiflayni min raḥmatihi wa-yajʿal lakum nūran tamshūna bihi wa-yaghfir lakum wa-llāhu ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: O you who believe, fear God and believe in His Messenger! He will give you a double share of His mercy, grant you a light by which you walk, and forgive you — God is Forgiving, Compassionate,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: this verse came down for some People of the Book who became Muslims, to whom God promises two shares (kiflān): a share for faith in God, another for their faith and assent to the messengers. And the light “by which you walk” prolongs the light of verse 12: no longer only at the Resurrection, but here below already, an inner clarity that guides each step.

mots.racine: و ق ي / أ م ن
mots.sens: fear God and believe in His Messenger
mots.def: ittaqā: to fear; āmana: to believe.

mots.racine: ك ف ل / ر ح م
mots.sens: He gives you a double share of His mercy

mots.racine: ج ع ل / ن و ر / م ش ي
mots.sens: He grants you a light to walk by

versets.ar: لِّئَلَّا يَعْلَمَ أَهْلُ الْكِتَابِ أَلَّا يَقْدِرُونَ عَلَىٰ شَيْءٍ مِّن فَضْلِ اللَّهِ ۙ وَأَنَّ الْفَضْلَ بِيَدِ اللَّهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَن يَشَاءُ ۚ وَاللَّهُ ذُو الْفَضْلِ الْعَظِيمِ
versets.tr: li-allā yaʿlama ahlu l-kitābi allā yaqdirūna ʿalā shayʾin min faḍli llāhi wa-anna l-faḍla bi-yadi llāhi yuʾtīhi man yashāʾu wa-llāhu dhū l-faḍli l-ʿaẓīmi
versets.trad: so that the People of the Book may know that they have no power over anything of God's grace, and that grace is in the hand of God; He gives it to whom He wills, and God is the Possessor of immense grace!
themes: grace in the hand of God
versets.commentaire: The sūra closes on the affirmation that grace (faḍl) is wholly in the hand of God — “the hand” meaning here power. Qushayrī draws the sūra's final indication: fear God by keeping adab with Him, and do not feel safe from His stratagem, lest He strip from you what He has given you of your moments. Be on guard against the surprises of His decree, lest He change what He has made you taste of the intimacy of His love. Follow the messengers, until He gives you two shares of His grace: preservation (ʿiṣma) and blessing (niʿma) — preservation is not to subsist by yourself apart from Him; blessing is to subsist by Him.

mots.ar: أَلَّا يَقْدِرُونَ عَلَىٰ شَيْءٍ مِّن فَضْلِ اللَّهِ
mots.tr: allā yaqdirūna ʿalā shayʾin min faḍli llāh
mots.racine: ق د ر / ف ض ل
mots.sens: that they have no power over God's grace
mots.def: qadara: to have power over; faḍl: grace.

mots.ar: وَأَنَّ الْفَضْلَ بِيَدِ اللَّهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَن يَشَاءُ
mots.tr: wa-anna l-faḍla bi-yadi llāhi yuʾtīhi man yashāʾ
mots.racine: ف ض ل / ي د ي
mots.sens: grace is in God's hand, He gives it to whom He wills

mots.racine: ف ض ل / ع ظ م
mots.sens: God is the Possessor of immense grace
